value_accrual_summary: A 0.2% on-chain tax on LUNC transfers burns 50% of collected fees and distributes the remaining 50% to stakers/validators, but staking yield is also supplemented by inflationary block rewards (community-pool and oracle rewards funded by new emission), making real fee-only yield negligible given extremely low on-chain volume; economic value capture is therefore minimal and largely theoretical.

_notes.burn_rate: 50% of the 0.2% on-chain transfer tax is burned; the other 50% goes to the community pool / staking rewards. Governance proposal #11111 (Commonwealth) set the tax to 0.2% in 2023.
_notes.staking_apr: Live APR is NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER from a single authoritative free source; CoinGecko does not publish it directly, and Station wallet figures fluctuate. Estimates seen on community dashboards range 3–8% annualized but are dominated by inflationary oracle/block rewards, not fee revenue.
_notes.inflation: Terra Classic retains Cosmos-SDK inflationary minting for block rewards (mint module). The mint module was NOT disabled post-collapse; annual inflation is governed by the mint module parameters and remains non-zero, confirming staking_emission_inflationary=true.
_notes.volume_caveat: On-chain LUNC transfer volume is very low post-collapse, so actual fee burn and fee-to-staker distributions are economically small despite the mechanism existing in code.
